title: aira caryophyllea var. latigluma
intro: Aira caryophyllea var. latigluma, also known as the Tufted Hair-grass, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the grass family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Aira caryophyllea var. latigluma include its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
